_comment: Scripted-brain fixture for the keyless e2e suite (design docs/keyless-e2e-tests-design.md §3.1). Loaded by KILN_BRAIN_MODE=scripted / KILN_BRAIN_SCRIPT. Each rule matches a pass's rendered round-1 context (transcript + event); rounds[i] is the canned response for the i-th LLM round. ${ticket} is substituted with the ticket id recovered from the tool results / event text, so a two-step create-then-ready works with no known-ahead id. See internal/brain/scripted.

rules._comment: keyless-core-loop.spec.ts: a login-form request creates a ticket and marks it ready (two rounds), then ends the turn.

input.title: Build a login form
input.body: Build a login form and wire it to the auth endpoint.

rounds.text: On it — I opened a ticket and marked it ready for a worker.

rules._comment: keyless-core-loop.spec.ts return leg: when the mock agent's turn completes, Kiln reacts by saying a line and posting a feed update. It does NOT accept-to-done — the merge gate verifies a real origin/main commit, which a keyless stack's disabled repo shell cannot satisfy (design §Test 1).

rounds.text: The worker finished its first turn on the login form.

input.text: The worker finished a turn on the login form.

rounds.text: Standing by for the next step.

rules._comment: keyless-voice.spec.ts: a spoken (mock-STT) request to add a dark-mode toggle creates a ticket and replies on screen.

input.title: Add a dark mode toggle
input.body: Add a dark mode toggle to the settings screen.

rounds.text: Added a ticket for the dark mode toggle.

rules._comment: keyless-notification.spec.ts / accept flow: when the user taps Accept on a proposal, handleAccept posts a 'tapped Accept … Mark that ticket ready now' message; Kiln marks it ready.

rounds.text: Accepted — queued it for a worker.
